In just a couple of hours, I used AI to make a cool Python console game.

Here's how I did it:
* I put my simple game ideas into ChatGPT
* ChatGPT made me a complex prompt that you can see in prompt.txt
* I put the prompt into Cline
* It coded the game for me!
* I asked it to make more and more ideas, fixed bugs, and even changed some things manually to make the game fun. (Only took five total prompts!)
* The craziest part is the rest of this Markdown file was generated by ChatGPT! (Mostly. There were a lot of parts that didn't make sense so I spent a lot of time editing it.)

This is my little project to see what AI could build, and the results actually surprised me (it was a fun game.) Have fun with my project!

*Also, if you are struggling in the game there are three tips in this doc that will help you beat it. Enjoy!*

# DungeonGame

*A text-based dungeon crawler written in Python.*

Fight monsters, collect treasure, grow stronger, and uncover the secrets hidden deep within the dungeon. Every floor presents new challenges, and only the strongest adventurers will survive long enough to confront the Cave King.

## Prequisites and Controls

The following libraries are needed for this game to function. Everyone usually should have these as they are built-in modules into Python. In fact, installing them with pip will result in an error.
- random
- time
- os


Menus and navigation in the game are entirely number-based.

Example:

```text
1. Attack
2. Special
3. Item

>> Your choice:
```

Simply enter the number corresponding to your choice.

## Features

- Turn-based combat
- Rest sites and training
- Merchant shops
- Inventory system
- Random item drops
- Character progression
- Boss battles
- Secret route and hidden ending
- Legendary weapon reward
- Multiple outcomes

## Starting Stats

| Stat | Value |
|--------|--------|
| HP | 10 |
| STR | 2 |
| DEF | 2 |
| Weapon | Training Sword |
| Gold | 0 |
| Inventory | Health Potion |

## Weapons

Weapons add bonus damage to attacks.

- Training Sword
  - Damage Bonus: +0 to +2
  - Starting weapon

- Sturdy Axe
  - Damage Bonus: +2 to +4
  - Obtained after Floor 1

- Knight's Blade
  - Damage Bonus: +4 to +6
  - Purchased from merchants

- Magic Staff
  - Damage Bonus: +3 to +8
  - Purchased from merchants

- Excalibur
  - Damage Bonus: +15 to +20
  - Secret reward

## Items

- Health Potion
  - Restores 25% of maximum HP.

- Sleeping Gas
  - 67% chance to put the enemy to sleep.
  - Grants 2 extra actions.

- Grenade
  - 80% chance to damage the enemy.
  - Deals 25% of current HP.
  - 20% chance to backfire and damage the player instead.

- Golden Apple
  - Restores 75% of maximum HP.

- Mysterious Key
  - Required to access the hidden route.
  - Does nothing in battle.

## Combat

During battle, players may choose between three actions.

**Attack**

Attack with your equipped weapon.

```text
STR + weapon bonus - enemy DEF
```

*Minimum damage is always 1.*

**Special Attack**

Special Attacks have a 50% success rate.
They deal triple your strength damage, and does not use the weapon damage.

```text
STR × 3
```

*Missed attacks deal no damage.*

*(Tip: A good idea in the game would be to always upgrade your STR so you could boost your Special Attack damage.)*

**Item**

Use consumable items during combat.

## Enemies

**Normal Enemies**
- Rat
  - HP: 8
  - STR: 2
  - DEF: 1

- Goblin
  - HP: 12
  - STR: 3
  - DEF: 2

- Skeleton
  - HP: 15
  - STR: 5
  - DEF: 3

- Orc
  - HP: 20
  - STR: 8
  - DEF: 5

**Elite Enemies**

Elite enemies are stronger and provide better rewards.

- Goblin Captain
  - HP: 14
  - STR: 4
  - DEF: 3

- Skeleton Knight
  - HP: 20
  - STR: 5
  - DEF: 4

- Orc Warlord
  - HP: 30
  - STR: 5
  - DEF: 5

## Final Boss: The Cave King

The ruler of the dungeon.

**Stats**
- HP: 50
- STR: 6
- DEF: 5

**Special Ability**

Crushing Blow
- 25% chance to perform a double-damage attack.

## Character Progression

Winning battles rewards the player with:

- Gold
- Random item drops
- Stat upgrades

**Normal Battles**

Choose one:
- +2 Max HP
- +1 STR
- +1 DEF

**Elite Battles**

Choose two:
- +4 Max HP
- +2 STR
- +2 DEF

## Merchants

Shops appear on Floors 5 and 8.

Available items include:

- Health Potions
- Sleeping Gas
- Grenades
- Golden Apples

*(Tip: Unlocking the Knight's Blade would be crucial to going forward. The Floor 5 Merchant sells it, requiring 20 gold, which should be obtainable in the previous levels. Also, the Floor 8 Merchant sells the Magic Staff.)*

## Rest Sites

Rest sites allow players to:

**Sleep**

Restore HP to full.

**Train**

Choose one:
- STR +1
- DEF +1

**Ritual (Floor 3 only)**

Perform a strange ritual.

Effects:

- Lose 3 HP
- Receive the Mysterious Key

## Dungeon Floors

| Floor | Events |
|---------|---------|
| 1 | Normal *(tutorial floor)* |
| 2 | Normal, Elite |
| 3 | Normal, Rest |
| 4 | Normal or Elite |
| 5 | Normal, Merchant |
| 6 | Normal, Elite, or Rest |
| 7 | Normal, Elite, Locked Door |
| 8 | Normal, Merchant, Rest |
| 9 | Normal, Elite, Locked Door |
| 10 | Boss |

- Normal = Fights one of the Normal enemies.
- Elite = Fight one of the Elite enemies. Harder but more payout.
- Rest = In the rest, you effectively skip fighting on that floor. You are given two choices: sleep (heals all health), or train (upgrade +1 STR or DEF).
- Merchant = Be able to buy stuff from it (also skips the level). Some things could only be bought from some Merchants.
- Locked Door = Some doors work, some doors don't. *Wonder how you unlock it?*

## Victory

Defeat the Cave King (Floor 10) and escape the dungeon.

Then you will be one of the few people to beat this hard game!

*(Tip: The final game-winning tip to beating this game would be to unlock the door. This will let you meet a helper that will boost you through the final floor. DO NOT give up even if some along the way doesn't work. It's intentional game design.)*

## Possible Future Ideas (To Ask AI)

- Save and load system
- More weapons
- Armor and equipment slots
- Additional bosses
- Status effects
- More floors
- Multiple endings
- Procedurally generated dungeons

## Good luck, adventurer.

**Will you escape the dungeon, or become another lost soul claimed by the Cave King?**
